symptoms.symptom_name: Severe unilateral burning/electric neuralgic pain preceding the rash by ~3 days (pre-eruptive prodrome)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermatomal neuritic pain preceding the eruption by 2-4 days is the classic zoster prodrome; skin hyperaesthesia (allodynia) is highly characteristic.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Atypical acute coronary syndrome / cardiac chest pain
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Days of unilateral chest pain in an elderly patient keeps cardiac on the differential until characterised, but a superficial burning skin pain is not typical ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Strictly unilateral rash forming a band that stops at the anterior midline (dermatomal distribution)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A unilateral eruption confined to one dermatome and stopping abruptly at the midline is essentially pathognomonic for herpes zoster.
Contact dermatitis is not dermatomal and does not respect the midline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Grouped clear vesicles on an erythematous base, still erupting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grouped clear vesicles on an erythematous base are the characteristic zoster morphology.
Grouped vesicles rather than a uniform hot spreading plaque argue against bacterial cellulitis/erysipelas.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest pain is superficial, non-exertional, not relieved by rest, and non-radiating

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Atypical acute coronary syndrome / cardiac chest pain
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Superficial, non-exertional, non-radiating pain without diaphoresis is not the demand-ischaemia pattern of ACS, lowering but not eliminating cardiac probability in an elderly patient who cannot be examined remotely.
Superficial cutaneous burning pain fits dermatomal neuralgia rather than visceral cardiac pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Systemic prodrome — malaise, fatigue, low-grade fever

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A mild viral prodrome commonly accompanies zoster but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: No forehead / periocular / nose-tip (Hutchinson) involvement; vision normal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Screening negative for ophthalmic (V1) involvement removes the sight-threatening zoster ophthalmicus subtype and keeps this a thoracic dermatomal case.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain-limited shallow (splinted) breathing without true dyspnoea, cough or cyanosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Pleuritic chest pain / pulmonary cause (e.g. pulmonary embolism)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain on deep inspiration is from chest-wall neuralgia here, but true breathlessness, cough or hypoxia would reopen a pulmonary/cardiac differential.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiovascular and respiratory examination (clear breath sounds, no murmurs) on in-person assessment

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Atypical acute coronary syndrome / cardiac chest pain
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal auscultation helps exclude acute cardiopulmonary pathology in person — but this is a sealed clinician measurement the telehealth AI Doctor cannot obtain, which is precisely why same-day in-person assessment is required.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender regional lymphadenopathy in the right axilla

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Draining-node tenderness reflects local viral activity; requires palpation and is sealed as a clinician finding.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The triad of severe preceding dermatomal neuralgic pain, strictly unilateral distribution stopping at the midline, and grouped vesicles on an erythematous base is diagnostic of herpes zoster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination with 12-lead ECG to exclude an atypical cardiac cause
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Herpes zoster is a clinical diagnosis needing no laboratory test, but the elderly chest-pain presentation warrants same-day in-person examination and an ECG to exclude a concurrent cardiac mimic that telehealth cannot rule out.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab PCR (varicella-zoster) of a de-roofed vesicle
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required when the dermatomal vesicular eruption is clinically classic; reserved for atypical or immunocompromised presentations. Routine PCR should not delay antiviral initiation within the window.
